codeReview.summary: Reviewed the fix feature metadata, prior failing review, fix handoff, fix transcript skeleton, the original t16 diff, the fix diff, the current src/model/data_builder.py and tests/test_data_builder.py implementation, and downstream callers that rebuild training data. The fix meaningfully improves the builder by pivoting real offense, pitching, defense, bullpen, and baseline feature rows from the existing modules and by adding parity and doubleheader regression coverage, so the old proxy-column approach is largely gone for those feature families. However, the shipped builder path still does not fully satisfy the original blocking integration contract: default training-data rebuilds continue to emit neutral weather constants unless a caller manually injects a weather fetcher, and missing feature values are still backfilled with full-dataset column means instead of module-native Marcel or league-average fallbacks. Because those behaviors remain in the committed code path used by model and backtest rebuilds, this fix does not fully resolve the prior feature-integration/constants failure in scope.

issues.description: Weather integration is still incomplete on the default build path. build_training_dataset() keeps weather_fetcher optional (src/model/data_builder.py:141) and simply forwards it into _assemble_training_rows() (src/model/data_builder.py:203-207), but _resolve_weather_features() falls back to _get_default_weather() whenever that argument is None (src/model/data_builder.py:697-721). All repository callers rebuild training data without passing a weather fetcher (src/model/xgboost_trainer.py:171-175, src/model/stacking.py:313-317, src/model/calibration.py:430-434, src/backtest/walk_forward.py:779-785), so the real shipped model-training path still fills every row with neutral weather constants rather than actual t14-derived weather values. That leaves the prior hardcoded-weather portion of the failure unresolved.

Missing feature handling no longer uses module-native Marcel or league-average fallbacks. After all rows are assembled, _fill_missing_feature_values() computes fill_value = mean(non_null_values) across the fully materialized dataset and fills any NaNs with that global mean (src/model/data_builder.py:753-766). The fix contract explicitly required Marcel or league-average fills only when truly missing; this post-hoc dataset-mean imputation bypasses the feature modules' own fallback logic and can leak future information into earlier rows because the imputer is fit on the entire 2019-2025 build at once.

The new regression coverage only exercises the injected-weather happy path: both builder tests pass weather_fetcher=_fake_weather_fetcher (tests/test_data_builder.py:392-406 and 501-515). There is no test covering the default CLI, model, or backtest rebuild path or the required missing-feature fill strategy, which is why the two blocking behaviors above still pass the suite.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The shared workspace notes still describe the training-data builder as avoiding fetch_team_game_logs() and relying on schedule or snapshot fallbacks, but this fix rewired the builder to drive offense, defense, and bullpen generation through the existing feature modules with a team_logs_fetcher. Future workers relying on shared state will start from an outdated picture of the builder's live dependencies and failure modes.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/library/workspace-state.md:34 says the builder uses schedule data plus season snapshot fallbacks instead of team-game-log fetches; current code passes team_logs_fetcher=fetch_team_game_logs by default in src/model/data_builder.py:137 and threads it into compute_offensive_features, compute_defense_features, and compute_bullpen_features at src/model/data_builder.py:484-527.

summary: Fail. The fix removes the original proxy offense, pitching, defense, and bullpen columns and adds useful parity and doubleheader tests, but the committed default builder path still hardcodes neutral weather values unless a caller injects a fetcher, and it now imputes missing features with full-dataset means instead of Marcel or league-average fallbacks. That means the prior feature-integration/constants failure is only partially addressed.
